My flight is going to depart on Monday.
In my opinion, it is better to travel light.
I am going to check my visa early. Better safe than sorry.
I am going to travel to Seoul in November. My assistant is going to book the flight.
We are going to meet the rector on Wednesday. She is going to present the agenda.
I am not going to bring an interpreter. I am going to speak English on my own.
Are you going to apply for a visa? Yes, I am.
So, Maria Clara, I hear you are going to Seoul in November. Are you going to travel alone this time?
Yes, I am. I am going to travel alone, and I am going to speak English on my own.
That is wonderful. When is your flight going to depart?
My flight is going to depart on Monday morning. The arrival in Seoul is on Tuesday.
And your accommodation? Are you going to stay near the university?
Yes. My assistant is going to book a small hotel near the conference. In my opinion, that is easier.
Good idea. Do you have your visa already?
Not yet. Tomorrow, I am going to apply for my visa at the embassy. Better safe than sorry.
You are very organized. Are you going to present your work at the conference?
Yes, I am. I am going to present my work in English. I am a little nervous, but I am ready.
In November, I am going to travel to Seoul for a conference.
My flight is going to depart on Monday morning.
I am going to apply for my visa at the embassy. Better safe than sorry.
In my opinion, the hotel near the university is better.
Sorry, I don't understand. Could you repeat, please?

Hello, Maria Clara. This is Daniel, from the international office. I am calling about your trip to Seoul in November. Here is the plan. Your flight is going to depart from São Paulo on Monday, the tenth, at nine in the morning. You are going to arrive in Seoul on Tuesday afternoon. Your accommodation is a small hotel near the university, so you are not going to travel far to the conference. Tomorrow, I am going to send the full itinerary to your email. Please remember your passport and your visa. Better safe than sorry. Have a good evening, and see you soon.

Good morning, passengers. This is a boarding announcement for the flight to Seoul. In twenty minutes, we are going to start boarding at Gate Fifteen. Please have your passport and your boarding pass ready in your hand. This is a long flight, so keep your small luggage with you. The departure is on time, at nine o'clock. Thank you for your patience, and have a wonderful trip.

First, I am going to apply for my visa at the embassy. Then, I am going to book the flight and the accommodation. On Monday, I am going to depart from São Paulo. On Tuesday, I am going to arrive in Seoul for the conference.
